intitule: Conditional Sentences: Type 2 and Type 3 - Multiple Choice

enonce.texte: Choose the correct form to complete each conditional sentence.

questions.texte: If I ______ you, I would accept the job offer.
questions.reponse: were

questions.texte: If she had studied harder, she ______ the exam.
questions.reponse: would have passed

questions.texte: If I ______ more money, I would buy a new car.
questions.reponse: had

questions.texte: We ______ to the party if we had known about it.
questions.reponse: would have gone

questions.texte: If I were you, I ______ him the truth.
questions.reponse: would tell

questions.texte: He ______ the job if he had applied for it.
questions.reponse: would have gotten

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verbs in parentheses.

questions.texte: If I ______ (be) rich, I ______ (travel) around the world.
questions.reponse: were / would travel

questions.texte: If she ______ (know) about the meeting, she ______ (come).
questions.reponse: had known / would have come

questions.texte: If we ______ (leave) earlier, we ______ (arrive) on time.
questions.reponse: had left / would have arrived

questions.texte: If I ______ (can) speak French, I ______ (move) to France.
questions.reponse: could / would move

questions.texte: He ______ (not / make) that mistake if he ______ (listen) to his teacher.
questions.reponse: would not have made / had listened

questions.texte: If they ______ (have) a car, they ______ (drive) to the beach.
questions.reponse: had / would drive

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions using type 2 and type 3 conditionals.

Text: The Missed Opportunity

Maria is a talented musician. She studied music in college and dreamed of becoming a famous pianist. However, after graduation, she decided to become a teacher instead. She thought it was a safer career choice. Now, ten years later, she often wonders what her life would be like if she had pursued music professionally. She sometimes imagines herself performing in big concert halls. She also thinks about how different her life would be if she had moved to New York, where many musicians go to start their careers. Her friends tell her it's never too late to follow her dream. But Maria feels that now she is too old to start a new career. She often says, "If I were younger, I would try again." Her students don't know that their teacher is a frustrated artist.
images: A woman looking at a piano with a thoughtful expression.

questions.texte: What does Maria wonder about her past decision? Use a type 3 conditional.
questions.reponse: Maria wonders what her life would have been like if she had pursued music professionally.

questions.texte: What does she imagine about her current life? Use a type 2 conditional.
questions.reponse: She imagines that she would be performing in big concert halls if she had moved to New York.

questions.texte: Analyze Maria's feelings about her current situation. Why does she feel she can't pursue music now?
questions.reponse: Maria feels that now she is too old to start a new career. She believes that if she were younger, she would try again.

questions.texte: If you were Maria, what would you do? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Die Schüler sollten ihre Meinung äußern, z.B. "If I were Maria, I would start playing piano again because it's never too late to follow your dreams."

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using type 2 or type 3 conditionals.

questions.texte: I didn't study, so I failed the test.
questions.reponse: If I had studied, I would not have failed the test.

questions.texte: I don't have a car, so I take the bus.
questions.reponse: If I had a car, I would not take the bus.

questions.texte: She is not rich, so she can't travel often.
questions.reponse: If she were rich, she would travel often.

questions.texte: He didn't take the job, so he didn't move to London.
questions.reponse: If he had taken the job, he would have moved to London.

questions.texte: I don't speak Italian, so I can't read this book.
questions.reponse: If I spoke Italian, I could read this book.

enonce.texte:
You are a career counselor. A student named Ahmed has come to you for advice. Read his situation and use type 2 and type 3 conditionals to answer the questions.

Ahmed's Situation:

Ahmed is 22 years old. He wanted to be a doctor, but he didn't have enough money for medical school. Instead, he studied business. Now he works in a bank, but he is not happy. He thinks about what his life would be like if he had gone to medical school. He also worries that it's too late to change his career. He wants to know what he should do now.
images: A young man sitting in an office with a serious expression.

questions.texte: What advice would you give Ahmed about his current situation? Use type 2 conditionals.
questions.reponse: If I were Ahmed, I would consider going back to school. If he saved money, he could take a part-time course.

questions.texte: What does Ahmed think about his past decision? Use type 3 conditionals.
questions.reponse: Ahmed thinks about what his life would have been like if he had gone to medical school.

questions.texte: Analyze Ahmed's options. What could he do to become happier in his career?
questions.reponse: Ahmed could take night classes, save money for medical school, or find a job in healthcare administration. If he worked in a hospital, he might feel more fulfilled.

questions.texte: If Ahmed had followed his dream, what do you think his life would be like now?
questions.reponse: If Ahmed had become a doctor, he would probably be happier, but he might also be very busy. He might have a lot of student debt, but he would be helping people.

enonce.texte:
Complete the dialogue using type 2 and type 3 conditionals.

Anna: I can't believe I lost my phone again!
Mark: If you ______ (be) more careful, you ______ (lose) it.
Anna: I know! If I ______ (not / put) it on the table, I ______ (still / have) it.
Mark: What would you do if you ______ (find) it?
Anna: If I ______ (find) it, I ______ (be) so happy!
Mark: If I ______ (have) your number, I ______ (call) you to cheer you up.
Anna: Thanks, Mark. If I ______ (not / lose) my phone, we ______ (can / talk) more often.

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the first blank:
questions.reponse: were

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the second blank:
questions.reponse: wouldn't have lost

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the third blank:
questions.reponse: hadn't put

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the fourth blank:
questions.reponse: would still have

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the fifth blank:
questions.reponse: found

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the sixth blank:
questions.reponse: would be

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the seventh blank:
questions.reponse: had

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the eighth blank:
questions.reponse: would call

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the ninth blank:
questions.reponse: hadn't lost

questions.texte: Choose the correct form for the tenth blank:
questions.reponse: could talk

enonce.texte: Each sentence contains an error in the conditional structure. Identify and correct the mistake.

questions.texte: If I would be you, I would accept the offer.
questions.reponse: If I were you, I would accept the offer. (Type 2 uses 'were' for all persons, not 'would be'.)

questions.texte: If she had knew the answer, she would have told us.
questions.reponse: If she had known the answer, she would have told us. (Past perfect requires the past participle 'known', not 'knew'.)

questions.texte: If they had gone to the party, they would enjoy it.
questions.reponse: If they had gone to the party, they would have enjoyed it. (Type 3 requires 'would have' + past participle in the main clause.)

questions.texte: If I had money, I will buy a new car.
questions.reponse: If I had money, I would buy a new car. (Type 2 requires 'would' in the main clause, not 'will'.)

questions.texte: If I was you, I would apologize.
questions.reponse: If I were you, I would apologize. (In Type 2, 'were' is used for all persons in the if-clause, especially in formal English.)

enonce.texte:
Read the scenario and answer the questions using type 2 and type 3 conditionals.

Scenario: The Pollution Problem

In a small town, the river has become very polluted. The pollution started five years ago when a new factory opened. The factory owners were careless with waste disposal. Now, the town has to spend a lot of money to clean the river. The fish are dying, and people can no longer swim in the river. The town council is discussing solutions. They could close the factory, but that would mean job losses. They could build a water treatment plant, but that would be very expensive. Everyone agrees that something should have been done to prevent the pollution.
images: A polluted river with dead fish and a factory in the background.

questions.texte: What might have happened if the factory owners had been more careful with waste disposal? Use a type 3 conditional.
questions.reponse: If the factory owners had been more careful with waste disposal, the river would not have become polluted.

questions.texte: What could the town do now to solve the problem? Use a type 2 conditional.
questions.reponse: If the town built a water treatment plant, it would cost a lot of money, but the river would become clean.

questions.texte: Analyze the dilemma the town faces. What should they do? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: The town faces a dilemma between the environment and the economy. If they close the factory, they will save the river but lose jobs. If they build a water treatment plant, they will protect jobs but spend a lot of money. I think they should build a treatment plant because it is a long-term solution.

questions.texte: What advice would you give to the town council? Use conditionals.
questions.reponse: If I were on the town council, I would try to find a compromise. If they worked with the factory owners, they might be able to reduce pollution without closing the factory. If they don't act now, the problem will get worse.
